title: knautia ovatifolia
intro: Knautia ovatifolia, also known as the shepherd's needle or saw wort, is a flowering plant in the family Caprifoliaceae, native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which have been attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting immune function. Knautia ovatifolia is typically prepared as a tea or tincture, and its herbal preparations are often used to treat coughs, colds, and other respiratory ailments.
